w:r.w:t: Full migration from MongoDB + Express to Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Storage + Realtime)
DRIPLENS currently uses MongoDB Atlas + Express + Cloudinary + manual JWT. Supabase gives you ALL of that in one platform, free-tier, with a much cleaner codebase. Here's the direct comparison:
Supabase runs backend — Vercel for frontend only
Single Supabase project URL + anon key
You keep the Express server for custom logic (file handling, role checks), but remove Mongoose, bcrypt, and Cloudinary entirely.
Go to https://supabase.com and sign in (free tier is all you need)
Click New Project, name it driplens-prod
Choose a strong database password — SAVE IT, you'll need it in .env
After project creation, go to Settings > API in the Supabase dashboard. You need:
anon public key — safe to expose in frontend
service_role secret key — NEVER expose in frontend, use only in Express server
Disable email confirmation (for dev) OR set up SMTP for production
Set JWT Expiry to 604800 (7 days, matching your existing config)
3. PostgreSQL Schema — Run in Supabase SQL Editor
Open Supabase Dashboard > SQL Editor and run these scripts in order. Each block is a separate query.
Supabase Auth handles passwords and sessions. We store extra fields in a profiles table that links to auth.users via foreign key.
id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
role TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN ('creator', 'brand')),
-- Auto-create profile row on signup via trigger
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user();
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
creator_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
media_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (media_type IN ('image', 'video')),
storage_path TEXT, -- Supabase Storage path for deletion
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
brand_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
creator_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
hiring_request_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.hiring_requests(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
sender_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES public.profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at();
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION public.update_updated_at();
RLS is Supabase's built-in access control. It replaces your auth.js middleware at the database level. Enable it and define who can read/write what.
ALTER TABLE public.profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.portfolio_items ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.hiring_requests ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE public.messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
-- Anyone can view any profile (public creator/brand pages)
-- Only the owner can update their own profile
-- Only creator who owns it can insert
-- Only creator who owns it can delete
-- Brand or Creator involved in request can read it
-- Both parties in hiring request can read messages
SELECT brand_id FROM public.hiring_requests WHERE id = hiring_request_id
SELECT creator_id FROM public.hiring_requests WHERE id = hiring_request_id
-- Either party can send a message
SELECT brand_id FROM public.hiring_requests WHERE id = hiring_request_id
SELECT creator_id FROM public.hiring_requests WHERE id = hiring_request_id
Go to Storage in your Supabase dashboard and create two buckets:
Public: YES — creators' work needs to be publicly viewable
Public: YES — profile pictures are public
-- Allow authenticated users to upload to portfolio-media
-- Allow owner to delete their own uploads
-- Repeat similar policies for 'avatars' and 'banners' buckets
Remove mongoose, bcrypt, jsonwebtoken, cloudinary, multer (from old stack) and add only:
# Supabase Anon Key (safe for client-side too)
# Supabase Service Role Key (NEVER expose to frontend)
// Public client — respects RLS, use for user-context operations
// Admin client — bypasses RLS, use ONLY for server-side admin operations
Replaces the old manual JWT verify. Supabase handles token validation:
// Verify Supabase JWT and attach user + profile to req
return res.status(401).json({ error: 'Missing or invalid token' });
const { data: { user }, error } = await supabaseAdmin.auth.getUser(token);
Supabase handles passwords and sessions. Our routes just call Supabase Auth:
const { username, email, password, role } = req.body;
return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Role must be creator or brand' });
// Supabase creates auth user AND triggers profile creation via DB trigger
data: { username, role } // Passed to trigger as raw_user_meta_data
6.6 server/routes/upload.js — Portfolio Upload to Supabase Storage
// Use memory storage — file goes directly to Supabase, never touches disk
router.post('/portfolio', requireAuth, requireRole('creator'), upload.single('media'), async (req, res) => {
if (!req.file) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'No file uploaded' });
const { data: storageData, error: storageError } = await supabaseAdmin
const { data: item, error: dbError } = await supabaseAdmin
// GET /api/creators — Public listing with optional filters
id, username, bio, location, category, avatar_url, banner_url,
const { data, error } = await query.order('created_at', { ascending: false });
id, username, bio, location, category, avatar_url, banner_url, created_at,
if (error) return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Creator not found' });
const { bio, location, category, avatar_url, banner_url } = req.body;
// POST /api/hiring — Brand sends request to creator
const { creator_id, project_title, project_description, budget } = req.body;
// GET /api/hiring — Get all requests involving current user
if (!content?.trim()) return res.status(400).json({ error: 'Message cannot be empty' });
res.json({ status: 'ok', platform: 'Supabase', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() });
app.listen(PORT, () => console.log(`DRIPLENS server running on port ${PORT}`));
Replace polling with Supabase Realtime subscription — messages appear instantly:
Go to Database > Replication in your Supabase dashboard and enable replication for:
Enable Realtime in the SQL Editor too: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.messages; ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE public.hiring_requests;
Push updated server/ code to GitHub
In Render dashboard, add all server .env variables
Build command: npm install | Start command: node index.js
In Vercel project settings, add VITE_SUPABASE_URL, VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, VITE_API_URL
vercel.json rewrite for React Router still applies (no change needed)
Vercel only — points to Render backend URL
SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY bypasses all RLS. Keep it server-side only in the Express backend. If this leaks, anyone has full DB access.
Create Supabase project, get URL + keys
Run all SQL schema scripts in Supabase SQL Editor (Section 3)
Create storage buckets: portfolio-media, avatars, banners (Section 5)
Enable Realtime replication for messages + hiring_requests
In server/: npm remove mongoose bcrypt jsonwebtoken cloudinary
In server/: npm install @supabase/supabase-js cors dotenv multer
Replace server files with code from Sections 6.3 to 6.10
Update MessagingPage.jsx to use Realtime subscription
Test locally: npm run dev in both client/ and server/
Deploy server to Render with new env vars
Deploy client to Vercel with new env vars
DRIPLENS — Built at AISSMS College of Engineering, Pune
Faculty Guide: Prof. N.D. Gaikwad | Team: Aayu · Annanya · Atharv · Aditi · Anandi
